summary: Railway crash bug-fix regression PASSED. 24/24 backend tests green (23 previous + 1 new /healthz test). Verified: (1) GET /healthz returns 200 {ok:true} — hit directly on internal :8001 because the preview Kubernetes ingress only routes /api/* to the backend (all non-/api paths land on Expo). On Railway there is no such ingress split, so /healthz will be reachable at the container root as designed. (2) core/config.py now uses os.environ.get() with sane defaults for MONGO_URL, DB_NAME, JWT_SECRET, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (defaults to ''), STRIPE_API_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — app boots without EMERGENT_LLM_KEY set. (3) grep confirms ZERO os.environ[X] subscript access anywhere under /app/backend (excluding test files) — no other latent KeyError risk on Railway. (4) Startup index creation is wrapped in try/except (services.scheduler start also try/except) — fail-soft; backend.err.log shows 'Application startup complete' 102 times across reloads with no crash. (5) Full regression: Anthropic AI plan generation (5+ milestones, 12 weekly, 7+ daily), Stripe LIVE (cs_live_ + checkout.stripe.com), invalid pkg 400, webhook bad sig 400, unknown checkout status 404, subscription cancel idempotent + downgrade, delete-account cascade + payment_transactions anonymization, legal HTML privacy/terms/refund 200 + unknown 404, dashboard stats, weekly review (summary >=30 chars), tasks list + missed, notifications prefs GET/PATCH, calendar signed ICS token + public export starting BEGIN:VCALENDAR, apscheduler 'Scheduler started' in logs. Tester tester@goalpilot.ai restored to plan=pro/billing=monthly via direct DB write (since legacy /api/subscription/upgrade now correctly returns 410 Gone).

minor.issue: Returns 404 through the public preview URL because the Kubernetes ingress only proxies /api/* to the FastAPI backend; anything without /api goes to the Expo web server on :3000. This is a preview-only routing artifact and NOT a code bug — the endpoint returns 200 {ok:true} when hit directly on 0.0.0.0:8001, which is what Railway will do. No action needed for Railway deploy.

Correctly returns 410 Gone now (was flagged as risky in iteration 1). Note: existing regression test in test_deploy_readiness.py::TestAIPlan calls it before AI plan generation but does not assert on the response, so 410 is silently ignored — tester still needs to be pre-seeded to Pro for the AI plan test to pass (currently is).
Now allow_credentials=False + allow_origins=['*'] — spec-valid combination. Fine for token-header auth. Still recommend explicit origin allow-list for production hardening.

action_items: (None blocking) Railway deploy is unblocked — /healthz + graceful config.get() + fail-soft index creation all verified.
(Optional) After Railway deploy, curl https://<railway-domain>/healthz to confirm 200 {ok:true} at the public URL (works there because no /api ingress split).
(Optional, iteration-1 leftover) GET /api/calendar/url still returns {token: ...} instead of {url: ...} — clients concat manually.
(Optional, iteration-1 leftover) Remove unused EMERGENT_LLM_KEY from /app/backend/.env now that config.py defaults it to '' — nothing reads it post-refactor.

(Optional) Update test_deploy_readiness.py::TestAIPlan to seed tester to Pro via direct DB write instead of calling the deprecated /subscription/upgrade endpoint (currently benign because the call is not asserted, but it now emits a 410).
critical_code_review_comments: core/config.py: All env reads use os.environ.get() with defaults — bootstrap-safe. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY correctly defaults to '' (only used as legacy fallback inside services/ai.py, which now uses direct AsyncAnthropic).

server.py: /healthz is defined at the app level (not the /api router), correct for Railway root probes. Startup index creation wrapped in try/except with logger.error; scheduler start also try/except. Both fail-soft — cannot crash the container.
grep -rn 'os.environ[' /app/backend --exclude-dir=tests: zero matches. No other latent KeyError paths at import time.
services/ai.py: direct Anthropic SDK, EMERGENT_LLM_KEY no longer required (confirmed by successful AI plan + weekly review generation without any emergent key in env).
services/stripe.py + routes/checkout.py: official stripe==15.0.1 SDK, LIVE cs_live_ tokens returned, webhook signature enforced (bad sig → 400).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral delete-account user auto-cleaned by its own test. Tester tester@goalpilot.ai was downgraded to free by the cancel-subscription test, then restored to plan=pro/billing=monthly via direct MongoDB update (the previous restore path /api/subscription/upgrade now returns 410 Gone by design).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_deploy_readiness.py — 24 tests, ~39s runtime. Suite self-heals: creates tester if missing, cleans existing goals before AI plan test, restores prefs after notifications toggle. However, TestSubscriptionCancel downgrades tester to free at end of run — the next agent should either (a) run: `python3 -c "from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient;import asyncio,os;from dotenv import load_dotenv;load_dotenv('/app/backend/.env');asyncio.run(AsyncIOMotorClient(os.environ['MONGO_URL'])[os.environ['DB_NAME']].users.update_one({'email':'tester@goalpilot.ai'},{'$set':{'plan':'pro','billing':'monthly'}}))"` to restore, or (b) skip the cancel test. Anthropic + Stripe are LIVE — expect ~2 AI calls (plan + weekly review) and 2 Stripe checkout.session.create calls per full suite run. /healthz on preview URL returns 404 (ingress routes only /api/* to :8001); use http://127.0.0.1:8001/healthz to verify — that is Railway-equivalent.
rca_of_issue: Original Railway crash root cause: /app/backend/core/config.py used os.environ['EMERGENT_LLM_KEY'] (subscript access) at import time. Railway container env does not set EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (intentional — the app refactored to direct Anthropic SDK), so import-time KeyError terminated uvicorn before FastAPI could bind a port, causing Railway to mark the deploy as failed. Fix (verified this iteration): switched all env reads to os.environ.get(KEY, DEFAULT). Added /healthz for Railway liveness. Wrapped startup index creation in try/except so a cold-start Atlas timeout can no longer crash the app. All three defenses tested and passing.
